title: My English Voice: Grade 5 Capstone Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A black rock burned for heat and energy
choices: coal
encounter
issue
rounds.answer: coal
rounds.explanation: coal: A black rock burned for heat and energy

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A set of rules that explains how a country or organisation works.
choices: coal
encounter
constitution
rounds.answer: constitution
rounds.explanation: constitution: A set of rules that explains how a country or organisation works.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A situation where people are unhappy and may protest or cause trouble.
choices: encounter
unrest
coal
rounds.answer: unrest
rounds.explanation: unrest: A situation where people are unhappy and may protest or cause trouble.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A small bag or pocket that can hold things
rounds.answer: pouch

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A special document that gives rights or rules to a group.
rounds.answer: charter

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A situation where people are unhappy and may protest or cause trouble.
rounds.answer: unrest

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: We use coal to help make electricity for our homes.
Our school has a policy that everyone must wear a uniform.
She was furious when her brother broke her favourite toy.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully.
Feeling happy and thankful for something nice someone has done.
Very angry or mad.
Worried or nervous about something that might happen.
rounds.answer: Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully.
rounds.explanation: He made a hasty decision to leave the game early.

choices: Easily annoyed or made angry.
Feeling happy and thankful for something nice someone has done.
Not wanting to do something or feeling unsure about it.
Worried or nervous about something that might happen.
rounds.answer: Not wanting to do something or feeling unsure about it.
rounds.explanation: She was reluctant to try the new food because she didn't know if she would like it.

choices: To convince someone to do or believe something.
To make someone believe that something is true.
To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
To share information or ideas with someone.
rounds.answer: To share information or ideas with someone.
rounds.explanation: Good writers communicate their ideas clearly.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.target: Explain three portfolio choices and support each choice with a specific example.
Our school has a policy that everyone must wear a uniform.
six audience questions in complete, relevant sentences.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Sarah shared a quote from her grandmother.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The rain was unexpected, so we got wet without our umbrellas.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The rural village was peaceful and surrounded by farms.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'hasty' mean in this unit?
choices: Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully.
A black rock burned for heat and energy
To meet or come across someone by chance
rounds.answer: Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully.
rounds.explanation: hasty: Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully.

rounds.prompt: What does 'unexpected' mean in this unit?
choices: A black rock burned for heat and energy
To meet or come across someone by chance
Something that happens suddenly and is not planned.
rounds.answer: Something that happens suddenly and is not planned.
rounds.explanation: unexpected: Something that happens suddenly and is not planned.

rounds.prompt: What does 'encounter' mean in this unit?
choices: A problem or topic that people talk about or need to think about
To meet or come across someone by chance
A black rock burned for heat and energy
rounds.answer: To meet or come across someone by chance
rounds.explanation: encounter: To meet or come across someone by chance

choices: Noun

choices: Adjective

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

pairs: To meet or come across someone by chance
A problem or topic that people talk about or need to think about
A plan or rule that helps people decide what to do.
Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully.
Worried or nervous about something that might happen.
To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
Something that happens suddenly and is not planned.
Related to a city or town.
The ability to learn, understand, and think about things.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

choices: A place or thing where something comes from
A problem or topic that people talk about or need to think about
A very bright light that is hard to see in
To show something that is usually hidden or to make something known
rounds.answer: A problem or topic that people talk about or need to think about
rounds.explanation: We had a big issue with our school project, so we worked together to fix it.

choices: A plan or rule that helps people decide what to do.
A set of rules that explains how a country or organisation works.
A special document that gives rights or rules to a group.
Related to making laws or rules.
rounds.answer: A plan or rule that helps people decide what to do.
rounds.explanation: Our school has a policy that everyone must wear a uniform.

choices: A large area inside a building where people wait or meet.
A special document that gives rights or rules to a group.
A special job or task that someone is trying to do.
Related to making laws or rules.
rounds.answer: A large area inside a building where people wait or meet.
rounds.explanation: We waited in the lobby of the museum for our guide.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

choices: A black rock burned for heat and energy
Areas or parts of a country with something in common
To meet or come across someone by chance
When the air has tiny water drops, making it hard to see
rounds.answer: A black rock burned for heat and energy
rounds.explanation: We use coal to help make electricity for our homes.

choices: A feeling of worry or nervousness
A small bag or pocket that can hold things
Areas or parts of a country with something in common
When the air has tiny water drops, making it hard to see
rounds.answer: A small bag or pocket that can hold things
rounds.explanation: He kept his coins in a small leather pouch.

choices: A large area of land that is controlled by one ruler or government
A very bright light that is hard to see in
The ability to take care of yourself and make your own choices
To show something that is usually hidden or to make something known
rounds.answer: The ability to take care of yourself and make your own choices
rounds.explanation: On her birthday, she felt proud of her independence because she could choose her own cake.
